Or, alternatively, “It is about half as wide as Earth or twice as wide as the Moon.” I point up the distinction (inherent in the conjunctional phrase “but also”) between present geological activity and past volcanism by saying “that are extinct,” and I add “as well as” to set apart the reference to canyons from the other remarks, as it doesn’t fit the “but also” phrase. use [[Mars Orbiter Mission]] instead of HST or Rosetta's pic because the camera on board the probe is practically the same as a normal camera (operates in 0.4 to 0.7 µm with 3 regular RGB channels in Bayer pattern, see https://www.researchgate.net/publication/273259237_MARS_COLOR_CAMERA_ONBOARD_MARS_ORBITER_MISSION_INITIAL_OBSERVATIONS). HST and Rosetta's cameras are more specialized and use ultraviolet channels in Mars images, which made them false color.
